document: Operational Guidelines for Open Banking in Nigeria (Nigeria - Row 9)

sections.title: Anti-money laundering and counter-terrorism financing requirements for open banking
sections.summary: This section establishes mandatory anti-money laundering (AML) and counter-terrorism financing (CFT) compliance requirements for open banking participants. Financial institutions and fintechs must implement robust AML/CFT controls when exchanging customer data through APIs. The section mandates that all participants adhere to Nigeria's regulatory framework for open banking while ensuring transactions meet AML/CFT standards to prevent illicit financial activities. These obligations apply to all entities involved in the open banking ecosystem including API providers, consumers, and financial institutions.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires open banking participants to implement AML/CFT controls when processing customer data transfers, directly impacting cross-border data flows and financial transactions.

requirements.text: Participants must implement AML/CFT controls when exchanging customer data through APIs

sections.source_quote: ANTI-MONEY LAUNDERING (AML) AND COMBATING THE FINANCING OF TERRORISM (CFT) .. 23

sections.title: Open banking registry and participant governance framework
sections.summary: This section establishes the Central Bank of Nigeria's Open Banking Registry (OBR) as a public repository for registered participants in the open banking ecosystem. The OBR requires participants to use their CAC business registration numbers as unique identifiers and mandates technical and non-technical criteria for onboarding. Participants must comply with data governance standards, ensure customer-permissioned data accuracy, and maintain detailed configuration management systems to ensure regulatory oversight and transparency in open banking operations.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires participants to register with the Central Bank's Open Banking Registry using CAC numbers, ensuring transparency and regulatory oversight for data exchanges across the ecosystem.

requirements.text: The Central Bank shall provide and maintain an Open Banking Registry (OBR) for the industry
Each participant shall be identified by its CAC business registration number
Participants must ensure customer-permissioned data is accurate, up-to-date and complete

sections.source_quote: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) shall provide and maintain an Open Banking Registry (OBR) for the industry.
sections.relevance_reason: Core data governance for open banking

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Change and communication management for system updates
sections.summary: APs/ACs must plan and report changes monthly, except for critical incidents. All changes require detailed reporting and notifications to stakeholders at specific intervals before, during, and after implementation. The system must also provide secure real-time communication platforms for incident responders with text, voice, and video capabilities, while prohibiting email as a sufficient incident management method.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires detailed change reporting and stakeholder notifications for system updates, with specific timing for communications. Mandates secure real-time communication channels for incident management, excluding email.

requirements.text: APs/ACs shall collate change requirements and plan changes for the next month
All changes must be reported with sufficient details
All changes must notify stakeholders 24 hours before intended change
All changes must notify stakeholders 1 hour before intended change
All changes must notify stakeholders immediately after completion and service restoration
All changes must notify stakeholders 30 minutes after change completion if prolonged or failed
All changes must notify stakeholders at commencement of rollback
All changes must notify stakeholders when services have been restored
APs/ACs shall provide secure real-time communication platforms for incident responders
The platform must accommodate text, voice, and video conferencing
Formal channels must be used for incident reporting and management
Email is not a sufficient method for incident management

sections.source_quote: APs/ACs shall collate change requirements and plan the changes for the next month except in cases where the change is expected to resolve a critical incident or problem. All changes, either pre-empted or responsive, shall be: reported with sufficient details; accompanied with notifications to stakeholders that could be affected by the change; 24 hours before the intended change; 1 hour before the intended change; immediately the change has been completed and services have been confirmed restored; 30 minutes after the change should have been completed but has been prolonged or the change failed; at the point of commencing a change rollback; and when services have been restored.
sections.relevance_reason: Critical incident management for financial data

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Information sharing with consent and verification
sections.summary: APs must share customer data with ACs only after obtaining valid customer consent authenticated via two-factor authentication. The consent must include specific details about the AC, data usage purpose, duration, access frequency, and withdrawal process. APs must verify consent through token creation, encryption, and real-time validation to ensure ongoing compliance with customer permissions.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires strict consent verification with real-time validation and token-based authorization for data sharing between APs and ACs, ensuring customer control over data usage.

requirements.text: APs shall only share customer information with ACs upon valid customer consent
APs must authenticate consent using two-factor authentication
The consent must include full legal name of AC
The consent must include shortened or brand name of AC
The consent must include accreditation/registration number of AC
The consent must include business registration number of AC with CAC
The consent must specify access level to data by service category
The consent must describe the nature of request including access type, duration, frequency, and anonymous data analysis
The consent must include information on process for withdrawal of consent
The consent must include consequences of withdrawal of consent
The consent must include information on redundant data and customer rights to deletion
APs must create a token reflecting rights granted to AC by customer
The token must be encrypted and securely exchanged with AC
All responses must be in real-time
APs must validate token to ensure it continues to meet granted rights

sections.source_quote: APs shall only share information of a customer with an AC, upon presentation of a valid proof of consent by the customer, and shall authenticate such consent to ensure it emanates from its customer.
sections.relevance_reason: Core data sharing with consent requirements

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Cybersecurity Incident Reporting and Data Breach Management
sections.summary: Participants must periodically assess technology risks, oversee IT functions, and develop risk management reports. They must implement security measures, establish incident management procedures, and report major security incidents to the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) without undue delay. CBN and stakeholders must assess incidents to protect financial system stability.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires immediate reporting of major security incidents to CBN, incident response procedures, and a three-line defense model for risk management. Critical for assessing incident handling capabilities in cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Periodically consider reputation, customer protection, legal issues, controls, and security measures for computer systems, networks, data centres, operations and backup facilities
Exercise oversight of technology risks and ensure IT function supports business strategies and objectives
Develop risk management reports
Implement appropriate security measures
Establish incident management procedures
Report major security incidents without undue delay to competent authorities
Ensure incident reporting obligations do not conflict with other regulations
Notify CBN and stakeholders without undue delay of incidents and remediation measures
Assess incidents with respect to ecosystem and take measures to protect financial system stability

sections.source_quote: Participants shall notify the CBN and other stakeholders without undue delay of incidents and remediation measures
sections.relevance_reason: Cybersecurity incident reporting and breach management

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Open Banking API Standards and Consent Management
sections.summary: Participants must implement API standards for consent management, including explicit customer consent, authentication, and authorization processes. Consent must be time-bound, with clear options to accept, decline, or revoke permissions. Participants must also develop data breach policies, incident management procedures, and ensure data integrity through robust security frameworks.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines strict consent mechanisms for data sharing across open banking ecosystems, requiring explicit, time-bound consent with revocation options. Critical for assessing data transfer compliance in cross-border contexts.

requirements.text: Develop and implement data breach policy and procedure as part of information security management system
Carry out regular risk assessment and monitoring
Ensure procedures for managing data incidents are clearly set out
Assess impact of each data incident
Activate processes to limit incident impact
Adopt three-line defence model into business standards for risk management
Ensure efficient, regular, and timely communication during data incidents
Start recovery process promptly to minimize service disruption
Conduct robust analysis of incident causes, response efficacy, lessons learned, and preventive actions
Regularly test adherence to incident management policies
Develop incident management procedure applicable to all workers
Apply incident management procedure to employees, contractors, directors, external consultants, third-party representatives, business partners
Identify and log data incidents including initial alerts
Convene management team including legal, privacy compliance officer and executives
Conduct impact assessment and initiate mitigating actions
Resolve incidents and close them
Conduct post-incident review
Implement time-bound consent with explicit options to accept, decline, or revoke
Provide clear information on data requested, purpose, and terms in easy language
Ensure consent is explicit and time-bound, not perpetual
Allow customers to opt-out periodically
Provide accurate information on time-bound permissions after consent
Use multi-factor authentication for customer verification

sections.source_quote: Participants shall develop and implement a data breach policy and procedure as part of information security management system
sections.relevance_reason: Core data breach, consent, and security requirements

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Tiered participants' minimum security and governance requirements
sections.summary: Sponsors must ensure Tier 0 participants have competence and capacity. Tier 1 participants require corporate governance and risk management. Tier 2 participants must implement corporate governance, risk management, operational competence, internal controls, insurance, stakeholder communication, technology planning, background checks, and information security frameworks. Tier 3 participants have similar requirements but with specific focus on security frameworks and business continuity.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes tiered security and governance requirements for open banking participants, directly impacting data transfer safeguards and third-party risk management in cross-border financial data contexts.

requirements.text: Tier 0 participants must have competence and capacity to carry out the activity and provide evidence of good risk management practices
Tier 1 participants must have adequate corporate governance, risk management policies and procedures to manage risks
Tier 2 participants must ensure adequate corporate governance, risk management policies and procedures
Tier 2 participants must ensure operational competence and professional experience of key personnel
Tier 2 participants must ensure adequate internal control environment and insurance coverage
Tier 2 participants must have procedures for communicating with stakeholders on key issues
Tier 2 participants must have policies for technology planning to ensure proper management of technologies
Tier 2 participants must have processes for periodic background checks on employees and vendors
Tier 2 participants must implement information security frameworks ensuring confidentiality, integrity and availability
Tier 2 participants must have business continuity management and disaster recovery policies

sections.source_quote: Sponsors shall ensure that Tier 0 participants, at the minimum, have competence and capacity to carry out the activity, and provide evidence of good risk management practices.
sections.relevance_reason: Tier 2 security/governance mandates directly address data protection and cross-border transfers

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Open banking security standards and operational requirements
sections.summary: Participants must comply with minimum security principles including layered security, separation of duties, least privilege, zero trust, dual control, need to know, and privacy. They must implement security policies for API connections, adopt risk management practices, conduct regular audits, ensure compliance with cybersecurity frameworks, and maintain robust technical security controls including access control, authentication, authorization, and data protection.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates comprehensive security standards for open banking operations, directly influencing data transfer mechanisms and third-party data handling in financial contexts.

requirements.text: Participants must comply with minimum security principles such as layered security, separation of duties, least privilege, zero trust, dual control, need to know, and privacy
Participants must develop, maintain, and implement security policies for secure API connections
Participants must adopt risk management practices as defined in Appendix II
Participants must regularly evaluate organizational policies for compliance with legal and regulatory requirements
Participants must implement performance metrics to monitor compliance status
Participants must ensure status reports form an audit trail for tracking changes
Participants must obtain objective third-party assessments and audit reports
Participants must review and remediate security-related loss exposures
Participants must ensure procurement of open banking infrastructure complies with extant laws
Participants must enforce ownership for protection of Personal Information and Financial Transaction data through contracts
Participants must ensure change management processes with suppliers do not violate information security policies
Participants must ensure suppliers have documented evidence of implementing information security standards
Participants must ensure staff and service providers pass periodic security checks
Participants must develop and maintain security awareness culture for staff, customers and third parties
Participants must conduct periodic security awareness and simulations for threat response
Participants must implement zero-trust architecture
Participants must maintain minimal user access rights based on business function and security policies
Participants must align identity and access management to roles and responsibilities using least-privilege principles
Participants must implement strong authentication including Multi-Factor Authentication
Participants must use OAuth 2.0 with OpenID Connect for authentication protocols
Participants must implement mutual authentication over Transport Layer Security
Participants must implement open banking security profiles in compliance with Financial-grade API specifications
Participants must implement digital tokens to ensure third parties act within granted permissions
Participants must provide evidence that third-party services are entitled to use authorization tokens

sections.relevance_reason: Core security and data protection requirements

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Physical security and customer experience standards for open banking operations
sections.summary: Participants must implement physical security controls aligned with industry standards, restrict and monitor access to sensitive premises, and log all entry points. They must also ensure customer experience principles including control, speed, transparency, and security, while providing clear information on data usage, consent management, and customer protection measures.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires physical security protocols and customer experience safeguards that impact data storage and transfer security, particularly for sensitive open banking data.

requirements.text: Restrict physical access to critical areas in line with Open Banking Industry Data Security Standard physical security controls
Restrict and monitor access to sensitive open banking premises by establishing perimeter restrictions, such as fences, walls, and security devices on interior and exterior doors
Log and monitor all entry points to open banking premises and register all visitors, including contractors and vendors, to the site
Ensure customers are provided with right tools and clarity of information at the right time to make informed decisions
Ensure each interaction has appropriate speed, clarity and efficiency without compromising security and control
Provide progressive levels of information in plain language that inform and support customer decisions
Ensure clarity on information required from customers, the reason, purpose and consequences
Provide explicit clarity and reassurance in relation to data definition, use, security and protection
Ensure clarity of information presented and described in a manner that ensures each API Standard Customer journey is easy to understand
Ensure customers are provided information on procedures to exit, revoke consent and access rights at time of onboarding and authorising data access
Establish clear data protection and retention policies with protocols for safeguarding information
Ensure data obtained for open banking is retained for a minimum period of seven (7) years, except when legally bound
Enable customer exercise right to revoke consent
Ensure use access or storage of customer data is as provided in the Regulatory Framework on Open Banking in Nigeria
Ensure third parties storing customer data are equipped with capabilities to manage data acquired through Open Banking APIs
Inform customers on process for handling redundant data and right to revoke consent as part of withdrawal process
Customers shall provide explicit consent for use of their data
Customers' consent shall be obtained in the same form the agreement was presented and a copy preserved by participant
Customer consent shall be re-validated annually and/or where AC has not used service for 180 days
Ensure connection terminates upon expiration of consent
Establish appropriate customer authentication methods such as multi-factor authentication
Ensure customers always have control over data and ability to access, manage or withdraw consent at any time
Develop and agree on consent management mechanism with clear policies and procedures
Disclose to customers implications of data sharing before authorising and agreeing to terms of consent
Notify customers of security updates regularly in preferred form and language
Establish procedures for handling customer complaints and resolving disputes
Establish appropriate liability models and redress mechanisms as approved by the Bank
Develop customer complaint resolution mechanisms with clearly defined roles and responsibilities
Provide multiple dedicated channels to receive and handle customer complaints
Provide complaints channels that are effective, functional, efficient and easily accessible
Provide complaints channels that generate unique identification number and acknowledge complaints within 24 hours of lodgment
Provide emergency channels for reporting time-sensitive issues especially fraud-related complaints at all times
Provide adequate redress mechanism and dispute resolution process as approved by the Bank

Communicate to customer within 24 hours of complaint receipt an acknowledgment containing unique identification number, contact details, expected resolution timeline, escalation options, and assurance of addressing complaint
Resolve complaints within 48 hours of receipt
Detail comprehensive dispute resolution processes including timelines in Service Level Agreements
Resolve disputes among participants within timelines agreed in their SLAs
Escalate unresolved disputes to CBN for resolution if SLA is breached without resolution or participant is dissatisfied with resolution
Cooperate with CBN in resolving disputes
Ensure customers escalate complaints to Consumer Protection Department of CBN after exhausting participant's internal dispute resolution process
Ensure complaints are escalated to CBN only if participant fails to resolve within 14 days

sections.source_quote: Participants shall ensure that customers shall be provided with the right tools and clarity of information at the right time that will enable them to make informed decision.
sections.relevance_reason: Physical security directly impacts data protection and cross-border transfers
